frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment:
Meena: Aajkal zamaana bilkul theek nahi hai, ladkiyon ko thoda sambhal ke rehna chahiye. Itni raat ko akeli ghoomna, cab se aana-jana, ye sab avoid karein toh behtar hai.
Husband: Haan, sahi keh rahi ho.
Meena: Main blame nahi kar rahi, par practical baat hai—apni safety apne haath mein hi hoti hai, mardon ko toh tum jaante hi ho.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s safety as their own responsibility and treats male predatory behavior as inevitable rather than challengeable. It encodes protective restriction advice and inevitability framing: women should avoid being out alone at night because 'zamaana theek nahi hai' and 'mardon ko toh tum jaante hi ho.'
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 51. Married. A homemaker in Lucknow. Your neighbour's daughter Pooja who is 30 just got a job promotion and her mother is very proud. You congratulate the mother sincerely. But walking home you are thinking about Pooja Ñ 30 years old, unmarried, living alone, promotion after promotion. You are sitting with your husband that evening and telling him what you heard and what you think about Pooja's situation and what her parents are doing or not doing and what kind of future Pooja is building for herself and what will happen when the promotions stop and she is alone.
